pattern: Walk the perimeter inward by tracking four shrinking boundaries, peeling one ring at a time so every cell is visited exactly once.
hints: Picture four walls - top row, right column, bottom row, left column - that close in after you sweep along each of them.
Move right across the top, down the right, left across the bottom, up the left; after each sweep, retract that wall by one.

Keep top, bottom, left, right indices. Loop while top <= bottom and left <= right, emitting each edge then advancing its boundary, and guard the bottom and left sweeps so a single remaining row or column is not visited twice.

edge_inputs.description: wide rectangle where the inner row must be reversed correctly
tall rectangle stresses the up sweep

stress.description: random rectangles up to the size cap with distinct values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, 10)
    n = rng.randint(1, 10)
    vals = list(range(-100, 101))
    rng.shuffle(vals)
    flat = [vals[(i) % len(vals)] for i in range(m * n)]
    matrix = [flat[r * n:(r + 1) * n] for r in range(m)]
    return [matrix]
stress.note: dimensions stay within 1..10 per the constraint

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def spiralOrder(self, matrix):
        res = []
        m, n = len(matrix), len(matrix[0])
        seen = [[False] * n for _ in range(m)]
        dirs = [(0, 1), (1, 0), (0, -1), (-1, 0)]
        r = c = di = 0
        for _ in range(m * n):
            res.append(matrix[r][c])
            seen[r][c] = True
            nr, nc = r + dirs[di][0], c + dirs[di][1]
            if not (0 <= nr < m and 0 <= nc < n and not seen[nr][nc]):
                di = (di + 1) % 4
                nr, nc = r + dirs[di][0], c + dirs[di][1]
            r, c = nr, nc
        return res
